input.title: The Algorithm
input.description: A documentary about three families suing a social media company after their children were harmed by algorithmically recommended content. Over 18 months, the film follows the lawsuit through depositions, expert testimony, and the families' daily lives — revealing how recommendation systems work and who is accountable when they fail.
input.comps: The Social Dilemma (Netflix, 2020), All the Beauty and the Bloodshed (HBO, 2022)
input.status: In production. 8 months of footage shot. Two families confirmed; third in negotiation.

input.title: The Beekeeper
input.description: A portrait of an 80-year-old beekeeper in rural Montana who has kept bees for sixty years and refuses to use modern chemicals.

input.title: Night Train
input.description: A documentary series following the overnight sleeper trains of Europe — Berlin to Paris, Vienna to Rome, Stockholm to Hamburg — and the people who ride and operate them. Part travel documentary, part social portrait, the series explores what happens when strangers share a confined space for twelve hours and what the revival of night trains says about Europe's relationship to time, distance, and sustainability.
input.comps: Slow Train Through Africa (Channel 4), The Trans-Siberian Railway (BBC)
input.status: Development. Director attached. Test filming completed on Berlin-Paris route. No financing in place.
input.awards: Project selected for Sunny Side of the Doc Forum 2026

input.title: The Water Wives
input.description: A documentary following women in three drought-affected villages in western India who walk up to eight kilometres daily to fetch water. Over two monsoon seasons, the film tracks how a community-led rainwater harvesting initiative changes — or fails to change — their daily reality. Part environmental film, part intimate portrait of labour that remains invisible in national policy debates.
input.status: Development. Director has shot a 12-minute proof-of-concept. Two villages confirmed access. No financing in place.

input.title: The Business of Death
input.description: A documentary investigating the funeral industry across four countries, exposing predatory pricing, emotional manipulation of grieving families, and the environmental cost of conventional burial practices. The film follows a whistleblower embalmer, a bereaved family pursuing a lawsuit, and an entrepreneur promoting alkaline hydrolysis as an alternative.
input.status: Research phase. Whistleblower source confirmed under anonymity agreement. No footage shot.

input.title: Nollywood Underground
input.description: A documentary series exploring the micro-budget filmmakers operating outside the mainstream of West African cinema — directors who shoot entire features on smartphones, distribute via WhatsApp and Bluetooth, and build audiences in markets and bus stations rather than cinemas or streaming platforms. Each episode follows one filmmaker from script to screening, revealing an alternative film economy that reaches millions without institutional support.
input.status: Development. Director has relationships with three filmmakers in Lagos and Accra. Test footage shot on two phones. Applied for Durban FilmMart project funding.

input.title: The Crossroads
input.description: An interactive documentary that places the viewer in the role of a climate policy advisor in a fictional island nation facing rising sea levels. At seven decision points, the viewer chooses between competing policy options — seawall construction, managed retreat, international litigation, geoengineering — and the documentary branches to show real-world footage of communities where each strategy was attempted. Every path ends with a documentary epilogue showing the actual consequences of that approach in a real location.
input.target_broadcaster: BBC iPlayer / BBC Three (interactive commissioning strand)
input.format: Interactive branching documentary, approximately 120 minutes total content across all paths, 40–50 minutes per viewing
input.status: Prototype. One branching path fully scripted. Technical proof-of-concept built. Two filming locations secured (Fiji and the Netherlands). No broadcaster attached.
